chunks.text: EECS Women In Technology take matters into their own hands - EECS at Berkeley The Women in Technology (WIT) Leadership Round Table , started by Prof. T su-Jae King Liu , EECS grad student Virginia Smith , EECS alumna Gitanjali Swamy and Sheila Humphreys is featured in Medium , an online community of writers, in an article titled “ Women in Technology: How a Handful of Leaders in Tech are Taking Matters into Their Own Hands ”, written by Virginia Smith. Aimed at developing sustainable solutions to increase the presence of women in technology, WIT is bringing together technology leaders in academia, industry, and non-profits to spark solutions-oriented discussion among women who can go back to their organizations and immediately make change. Categories Alumni Diversity Faculty Students View Open Faculty Positions
